memo: The packet describes Robin, an agentic system for therapeutic discovery. Implemented evaluation mechanisms: (a) an LLM-judged tournament that ranks generated drug candidates on scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and supporting-literature methodology (E03); its ranking output directly determines which candidates are proposed for wet-lab evaluation (E01, E03). (b) A validation study of the judge itself, comparing its preferences with human experts: 7.25/10 top-10 overlap and 88% vs 61% intra-rater consistency (E04). (c) Wet-lab experiments serve as an external evaluation signal fed back into the loop: humans conduct experiments and supply data, Finch analyzes, and Robin reinterprets results to generate a new candidate round (E02, E05) — an implemented feedback path from empirical outcome to later candidate selection. Human roles remain load-bearing: humans execute experiments (E05), translate outlines into protocols since Robin does not yet emit executable protocols (E06), performed demultiplexing and alignment (E07), and analyzed flow cytometry data (E08). Key risks: the judge is the sole gate on candidate ranking and its concordance benchmark uses the same expert-preference construct it is being measured against, with no independent ground-truth or calibration reported; and the system boundary between agent-executed and human-executed steps is described only in scattered method-level statements, so autonomy claims can be overread. No replication, calibration, persistent-memory, or formal-verification evidence appears in the packet.

diagnostics.statement: The LLM-judged tournament is the ranking mechanism that determines which drug candidates advance, and its reported validation is agreement with expert preference rather than any independent measure of candidate correctness.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.
the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10

diagnostics.consequence: Systematic judge bias shared with, or unmeasured against, expert opinion would propagate unchecked into which candidates are tested experimentally, since concordance with preferences cannot detect shared error.

diagnostics.statement: Robin is reported not to produce executable protocols, and several analysis steps in the reported workflow were performed by humans, so the boundary between agent-executed and human-executed work is stated only piecemeal.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.
while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols
Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

diagnostics.consequence: Readers and downstream integrators may over-attribute the reported results to autonomous agent execution and under-resource the human translation and analysis steps the loop currently depends on.

diagnostics.statement: The packet reports no independent replication of the experimental findings and no probabilistic calibration of evaluator confidence against observed correctness, despite the experimental results feeding a new round of candidate generation.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.

diagnostics.consequence: High self-consistency without replication or calibration can be mistaken for accuracy, and an unreplicated experimental result can be amplified across iterative candidate rounds.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.
the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10

success_check.observable: Rank correlation between the LLM judge's tournament ranking and an outcome-based criterion (measured experimental activity of the candidates) rather than expert preference alone.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Judge-vs-experimental-outcome agreement reported and exceeding the random-selection baseline the packet already uses for the top-10 comparison.
success_check.evidence_needed: Tournament rankings paired with wet-lab assay outcomes for the same candidate set, with the random baseline stated.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.
while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols
Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

success_check.observable: A single consolidated step-by-step attribution table marking each pipeline step as agent-executed or human-executed.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: 100% of reported pipeline steps (candidate generation, ranking, protocol translation, experiment execution, demultiplexing/alignment, differential expression, flow cytometry) carry an explicit executor label.
success_check.evidence_needed: The published pipeline description plus methods, checked step-by-step against the table.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.

success_check.observable: Replication of the key assay results before their interpretation is used to seed the next candidate round.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: At least one independent replicate run reported per key finding, with effect direction concordant with the original.
success_check.evidence_needed: Replicate assay data and a stated concordance comparison against the original run.
